bids.reasoning: At this early stage in the negotiation, there is no time pressure to accept a low-utility offer. The opponent's initial offer has the lowest possible utility for me given all issues' values have the least preferred utility scores. I will counter with a high-utility offer that reflects my preferences, sticking to my top preferred values for each issue to secure the highest possible utility.

Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.02), I aim to maximize my utility by maintaining a high-utility offer. The opponent's bids so far have featured the least-preferred values for each issue, which suggests they have not yet begun to make concessions or prioritize issues based on potential overlap in preference. I will continue to focus on the most preferred values for each issue, looking to gain leverage as more time elapses.

Current offers from the opponent are low in utility for me, sticking to less favorable values on highly weighted issues. Given only 4% of the timeline has elapsed, I will maintain a firm position on my preferences for issueA and issueB, as they're critical due to their high weight. I'll slightly concede on issueC and issueD to avoid stalling but still aim for a high overall utility, maximizing my interests while signaling slight flexibility.

Given the time fraction, I still aim to maximize my own utility while keeping an eye on potential concessions from the opponent.
